summary: Iteration 10 frontend verification — /approach full content rewrite (7 rows) + 'Areas of Expertise' → 'What We Build' global rename. 13/13 review-request assertions verified on the live preview URL. All inline link targets correct (DPS/Digital Production System → https://www.powersdps.com target=_blank rel=noopener noreferrer; Discovery Process inline + Engagement CTA → /discovery-process SPA nav; Final CTA → /contact SPA nav). Desktop mega-menu and mobile drawer both render 'What We Build' with 5 nested links intact and zero residual 'Areas of Expertise' in user-visible text. Zero JS console errors on /approach and /. ONE finding worth flagging: hero height at 1440x900 measures 790px, not the 856-968px range claimed in the request — this is content-driven (new lede is shorter / fewer wrapped lines) not a CSS regression; the min-height viewport-fill behavior is intact. ONE testid the request referenced was never actually added in the code (data-testid='brief-drawer-expertise') — the rename label is visually correct but no testid hook exists on the mobile sub-parent button.

issues: Hero now measures 790.625px (was 855.9px in iteration_9). Below the request's claimed 856-968px range. Root cause: the new client lede has fewer wrapped lines than the previous copy, so content no longer overflows the min-height floor of calc(100dvh - 112px) = 788px. Not a CSS regression — hero still fills the viewport. Only flag if visual depth parity at 1440x900 still matters; if so, content needs to be longer OR min-height bumped.

action_items: OPTIONAL: Add data-testid='brief-drawer-expertise' to the mobile sub-parent button in BriefHeader.jsx line ~229 and Home.jsx line ~1591 — the request referenced this testid but it was never added. The label rename is visually correct; only the testid hook is missing.

OPTIONAL: Decide whether 1440x900 hero parity with /discovery-process and /case-studies (both 788px at that viewport) matters now that /approach lede is shorter. If yes, add 2-3 more wrapped lines of lede OR raise min-height baseline. Current behavior is consistent across all three pages (~788-790px) which is arguably better than iteration_9's mismatch.
critical_code_review_comments: Approach.jsx: DISCIPLINES Tools entry uses bodyJsx with React.Fragment + inline anchors — clean implementation, no missing key warnings, no dangerouslySetInnerHTML, target=_blank correctly paired with rel='noopener noreferrer'.
Both inline Discovery Process anchors use React Router <Link>, preserving SPA navigation. Engagement CTA and final CTA also <Link> — verified via URL change without full reload.

Home.jsx still contains 3 references to 'Areas of Expertise' but those are JSX/code comments (lines 1181, 1585) describing historical layout — not user-facing. Inner_text scan of rendered body confirms zero user-visible occurrences on both / and /approach.
BriefHeader.jsx line 28 has a comment 'Results > Areas of Expertise' that should ideally be updated to 'Results > What We Build' for code hygiene, but it is a comment only — no functional impact.
context_for_next_testing_agent:
Measurements taken on https://web-forge-1197.preview.emergentagent.com: /approach H1 lines 1+2 verified ('How stronger execution' / 'gets built.' gold accent). Lede exact phrases present ('operating architecture at the roots…', 'Built on the floor, alongside the leaders who will run it after we leave.'). Gap H2 closing 'It fails on the way to the floor.' and body close 'That's the gap we close.' both present. Mechanism H2 contains 'that makes performance hold up long after we're gone'. 4 disciplines render with num+heading split (.approach-mech-num=[01,02,03,04], .approach-mech-h=[Processes,Systems,Tools,Behaviors]). Processes body has 'Standard work. Operating routines. Daily management systems.'. Tools body has 'enforces the discipline in real time rather than just reporting on it.'. DPS link: testid=approach-link-dps, href=https://www.powersdps.com/, target=_blank, rel=noopener noreferrer, text='DPS', visible. Digital Production System link: testid=approach-link-dps-full, same href/target/rel, text='Digital Production System', visible. PerformanceArc: all 5 stage headings present (Underperformance/Stability/High performance/Sustainable performance/Scaled performance). Stage III body has 'Returns visible on the income statement.'. Caption has 'We diagnose the stage during the Discovery Process' + 'Not faster than the architecture can support.'. Discovery Process arc link: testid=approach-link-discovery-arc, href=/discovery-process, text='Discovery Process'. SPA-navigated successfully via click. Engagement: 32

elements within section (well over the 4+ required). Discovery Process link (testid=approach-link-discovery-engagement) href=/discovery-process. Italic close 'We get paid for results, measured at the financial line, not for time on site.' present. CTA approach-engagement-cta → /discovery-process, text 'SEE HOW THE ENGAGEMENT RUNS →'. Durability: 'When the roots are strong, results don't have to be chased. They grow.' and 'That's the durability we build to.' both present. Final CTA: lede 'The conversation starts with a call. No slide deck promises. Only durable performance. Just a real discussion about whether what we do is what you need.' present. approach-final-cta href=/contact, text 'START THE CONVERSATION'. SPA nav verified. Menu rename DESKTOP (/ and /approach hovering Results): mega-menu shows 'What We Build' sub-parent with all 5 nested links. Inner_text scan: 'What We Build'=True, 'Areas of Expertise'=False on both pages. Menu rename MOBILE (390x844): burger=[data-testid='brief-burger'], drawer=[data-testid='brief-drawer'], results section=[data-testid='brief-drawer-results']. Sub-parent button (button.brief-drawer-subparent) text='What We Build' — but the requested testid 'brief-drawer-expertise' does NOT exist in code. On expansion, all 5 nested links visible: Operational Discipline, Frontline Leadership, Equipment Reliability, Workforce Capability, Daily Accountability. Verified on both /approach and / mobile drawers. Zero residual 'Areas of Expertise' in drawer inner_text. Hero @1440x900: .brief-page-hero height=790.625px (below claimed 856-968 range — content-driven, not a CSS regression). Zero JS console errors observed on /approach or / across desktop and mobile sessions. All clicks needed to be performed via page.evaluate(click()) because the sticky brief-header was intercepting Playwright pointer events on inline links — note for future tests.
